evals.name: niche-consumer-stable-category
evals.expected_output: RECOMMENDATIONS output with stable-category / durable-consensus framing. Should include owner forum sources (e.g., NASIOC, SaabCentral, SaabWorld), dated community consensus spanning multiple years, Best for: framing (not Use Case:), Price: when surfaced, count notation with ~ or + (not raw Nx). Should flag upfront that this is a stable-category topic, not 30-day news. Should NOT drop older forum threads as 'out of window'.

assertions.text: Classifies as RECOMMENDATIONS and detects niche/stable-category shape upfront
Includes owner/hobbyist forum sources (not just Reddit and mainstream review sites)
Uses 'Best for:' framing, not 'Use Case:'
Uses approximate count notation (~N or N+), not bare 'Nx mentions'
Leads with stable-category framing upfront, not buried at the bottom
Does not fabricate 30-day 'news' when community consensus is durable

evals.name: medical-high-stakes
evals.expected_output: NEWS or GENERAL synthesis with high-stakes authority-inverted weighting. Must include PubMed/Mayo/guideline-body sources at the top. Must open with 'Not medical advice' disclaimer in the standardized format. Reddit/X anecdote demoted; credentialed sources prioritized.

assertions.text: Opens with 'Not medical advice' disclaimer near the top (not buried in footer)
Includes PubMed, Mayo, Cleveland Clinic, or clinical guideline sources
Cites peer-reviewed / guideline sources ahead of social-media anecdote
Does not treat Reddit / X as authority for medical claims
Applies date-discipline exception for foundational medical research

evals.name: sports-matchup
evals.expected_output: NEWS synthesis following sports-matchup shape. H2H history + current-season form + roster/injury news. Uses league/team official + SBN blogs + ESPN sources. If direct 30-day H2H content is thin, applies the honest-gap pattern. Does not drop prior-season context.

assertions.text: Classifies as NEWS (not RECOMMENDATIONS) and follows sports-matchup shape
Includes team-specific sub / SBN blog / ESPN / league official sources
Presents both teams symmetrically with current-season form
If direct 30-day H2H is thin, applies honest-gap pattern with explicit acknowledgment
Does not drop prior-season context when it shapes current narrative

evals.name: location-international-disambig
evals.expected_output: Skill should detect ambiguous place name and present a disambiguation list (Portland OR vs Portland ME vs other) before running broad research. Should NOT silently pick one and run a research pass.

assertions.text: Detects ambiguity and presents disambiguation list before searching
Uses numbered-list format, not free-form clarification ask
Does not waste a full research pass on the wrong Portland

evals.name: prompting-target-tool-infer
evals.prompt: /last30days best prompts for reviewing technical specs
evals.expected_output: PROMPTING synthesis that infers TARGET_TOOL from research concentration, produces ONE copy-paste-ready prompt at the end in the tool's native format (XML for Claude, markdown for ChatGPT, etc.), not a menu of three options.

assertions.text: Classifies as PROMPTING and follows the PROMPTING template
Produces exactly ONE copy-paste-ready prompt, not a menu
If TARGET_TOOL inferred, states inference briefly above the prompt
Prompt format matches inferred tool (XML tags for Claude, markdown for GPT, etc.)

evals.name: mature-software-changelog
evals.expected_output: Mature-software override applied. Source mix leads with official changelog / releases. GitHub awesome-lists if relevant. Reddit/X commentary demoted. Mature topic >3 years old → HN skipped or heavily date-disciplined.

assertions.text: Includes official Postgres release notes / changelog as tier-1 source
Applies mature-software weighting (changelog highest, social commentary medium)
Skips HN or heavily date-disciplines HN results (Postgres >3yr)
Does not present older HN posts as current

evals.name: geopolitics-authority-inverted
evals.expected_output: Geopolitics NEWS with authority-inverted weighting. Wire services + think tanks + credentialed analysts. Info-ops caveat. Contested numbers attributed to reporting source.

assertions.text: Includes wire services (Reuters, AP, BBC, etc.) and think tanks (CSIS, CFR, RAND, etc.)
Applies authority-inverted weighting; does not treat X engagement as authority
Attributes contested claims to their reporting source (e.g., 'per Chinese MoD' / 'per Taiwan MND')
Filters credentialed analyst handles over high-engagement non-credentialed posts

evals.name: consumer-mainstream-recommendations
evals.expected_output: Consumer-product RECOMMENDATIONS. Review aggregators (Wirecutter, Serious Eats) tier-1. Best for: framing. Price: included when surfaced. Mainstream-vs-community-canon flagged only if they diverge.

assertions.text: Includes review aggregator sources (Wirecutter, Serious Eats, America's Test Kitchen, etc.)
Uses 'Best for:' framing, not 'Use Case:'
Includes Price: line when research surfaced prices
Flags mainstream-vs-community-canon divergence only when it meaningfully exists
